danger_signals.visual_form: Skinheads visibly holding knives, chains, or guns; motorbike enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat escalation; player must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive punk-style hair on Skinhead sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies hostile gang members; visual danger marker

danger_signals.visual_form: Skinheads entering melee distance, choking animation initiation
danger_signals.meaning: Critical threat state; player health in immediate jeopardy

danger_signals.visual_form: Junkyard debris, Brooklyn Bridge height, construction site crane/scaffolding
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger zones complement enemy threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Nunchaku items appearing on ground or dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary power increase; enhanced combat capability available

safety_signals.visual_form: Skinhead sprites disappearing from screen after defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Immediate threat neutralization; spatial safety restored

safety_signals.visual_form: Advancing through five distinct locations (street → junkyard → Brooklyn Bridge → back street → construction building)
safety_signals.meaning: Forward momentum indicates progress toward objective and final boss confrontation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Skinheads with knives, chains, motorbikes, guns; melee-focused variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Downtown New York City urban setting; junkyard territory; street-level infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Boss-tier enemy; implied authority over Skinhead gang
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Final stage boss enemy; presumably larger/more dangerous sprite than regular Skinheads
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Construction building rooftop; top location suggests power/dominance position

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Professional martial artist archetype; lone operative stance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Defending 'turf'; protecting hostage (Madonna); street-level urban justice

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Skinheads densely populate each stage; gang members control street, junkyard, bridge, and construction sites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies aggressively pursue player; initiate weapons use; attempt choking attacks to restrict movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Female hostage held by Giant Devil at final stage location; building under construction implies secluded/inaccessible location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must progress through five stages to reach final boss; each stage represents removal of another layer of gang control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player character advancing through gang-controlled zones; defeating enemies in succession
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies respawn across stages, indicating persistent gang threat; player must engage in multiple combat encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nunchaku can be picked up; when held, player sprite visually changes to show weapon use
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player armed vs. unarmed states; loss of weapon upon taking damage while holding nunchuku creates temporary vulnerability cycle

environmental_storytelling.example: Five stages move from street → junkyard → Brooklyn Bridge → back street → construction building, suggesting gang territory expansion and final stronghold location
Junkyard enemies may use chains/improvised weapons; Brooklyn Bridge enemies use guns (elevated tactics); construction site features height-based hazards and equipment
Downtown New York City 1980s urban decay aesthetics; Skinhead gang culture; construction/abandoned spaces indicate criminal underworld infrastructure
Giant Devil positioned at building-top (construction site roof) reinforces power hierarchy and final confrontation stakes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Advance toward player when in range; initiate melee attacks
Increased aggression radius; longer attack range; sustained threat
High-speed charging attack; mobile threat; stage-spanning presence
Projectile attacks from distance; forces player movement evasion; elevated threat tier
Initiate choking attack animation; attempt to restrict player mobility and deal damage
Enhanced combat capability; increased damage output and attack range
Weapon drop; automatic return to unarmed state; vulnerability window
Presumably heightened attack patterns; increased health pool; guards kidnapped Madonna

notes: Vigilante (1988) uses visual threat stratification through enemy sprite variation and weapon display to communicate danger levels. The five-stage progression structure physically manifests the narrative of gang territorial control being progressively dismantled. Enemy behavior is highly reactive and visually telegraphed—weapon visibility, proximity, and animation state all signal danger intensity. The Skinhead faction identity relies on distinctive punk aesthetics (mohawks, spiked hair) rather than color coding, making them visually cohesive despite sprite limitations of 1988 arcade hardware. The game communicates world-state primarily through enemy density, environmental complexity, and boss positioning architecture. Safety signals are minimal (weapon pickups only), emphasizing the hostile urban environment. The hostage narrative (Madonna) provides goal-state clarity but minimal visual manifestation until final stage.
